beginnerGuide.title: Kingdom Architect Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Kingdom Architect is a building and tycoon experience where placed structures generate progress and money. The main progression loop is to build, collect payouts, unlock additional structures, purchase more land, and improve the kingdom over time.

steps.title: Start With the Village Area
steps.description: Use the available starting space to create a compact village. Leave clear areas between important buildings so roads and future structures can be added without rebuilding the entire layout.

steps.title: Place Your First Buildings
steps.description: Begin with functional medieval structures such as houses and markets. Buildings contribute to kingdom progression and provide the foundation for earning money.

steps.title: Collect Building Payouts
steps.description: Check completed buildings for available payouts and collect them regularly. These earnings fund additional construction, upgrades, and land expansion.

steps.title: Unlock New Structures
steps.description: Continue building and earning to unlock more structure categories and upgrades. Add roads, towers, walls, gardens, terrain features, water, trees, and decorations as they become available.

steps.title: Purchase More Land
steps.description: Use accumulated money to expand beyond the original village area. New land provides enough room for larger districts, defensive walls, towers, and a central castle-style settlement.

steps.title: Use Offline Growth
steps.description: Kingdom progress and earnings continue while you are offline. Return regularly to collect accumulated rewards and reinvest them into the next stage of expansion.

steps.title: Finish the Castle City
steps.description: Connect districts with roads, define the outer kingdom with towers and walls, and finish unused spaces with gardens, trees, water, terrain, and decorative details.

quickTips: Build functional income structures before adding extensive decorations.
Leave at least one side of the village open so new land connects cleanly.
Collect payouts frequently and reinvest them into the next round of construction.
Offline growth keeps the kingdom advancing, so return regularly to claim rewards.

codes.title: Kingdom Architect Codes
codes.intro: Kingdom Architect does not currently publish any redeemable reward codes or list a code redemption system. Future codes and game announcements should be checked through the official Roblox experience and the Finite Works community.

items.reward: No code rewards are currently available
items.action: Follow the official Roblox experience and the Finite Works developer community for future code announcements.

items.reward: No previously released Kingdom Architect codes are listed
items.action: Newly released codes will appear here after they are officially published by the developers.

items.reward: No in-game code redemption option is currently listed
items.action: Open the game menu after major updates and look for a Codes, Gifts, Rewards, or Settings button.

bestBuildingsTierList.title: Kingdom Architect Best Buildings Tier List
bestBuildingsTierList.intro: This progression-focused tier list organizes the confirmed structure categories by their usefulness while growing a new kingdom. Economic buildings come first, followed by layout and castle structures, while detailed landscaping is best added after the main income loop is established.

tiers.label: Core Progression
tiers.bestFor: Establishing the village and supporting the money-earning progression loop.
tiers.strategy: Place these early and expand the economic section of the kingdom before committing large areas to decorative construction.

tiers.label: Layout Essential
tiers.bestFor: Connecting buildings and organizing future expansion.
tiers.strategy: Create a simple road network early so new districts can be added without leaving inaccessible or awkward empty spaces.

tiers.label: Castle Expansion
tiers.bestFor: Defining districts and turning the village into a fortified castle city.
tiers.strategy: Add walls and towers after securing enough land for the final kingdom boundary and major building areas.

tiers.label: Kingdom Detailing
tiers.bestFor: Landscaping, filling unused spaces, and completing the fantasy kingdom design.
tiers.strategy: Use these after the main buildings, roads, and expansion areas are established so decoration does not restrict progression.

moneyFarmingGuide.title: Kingdom Architect Money Farming Guide
moneyFarmingGuide.intro: Money is earned through the kingdom's buildings and continues accumulating through offline growth. The fastest practical progression route is to collect payouts frequently, reinvest in functional construction, unlock upgrades, and postpone extensive decoration until the kingdom has a stable economic base.

steps.title: Build the First Income Base
steps.description: Use the starting area for functional village buildings instead of filling it immediately with gardens and decorations.

steps.title: Collect Every Available Payout
steps.description: Move through the active part of the kingdom and collect completed building payouts before starting another construction phase.

steps.title: Reinvest Before Decorating
steps.description: Spend early earnings on additional buildings, newly unlocked structures, and useful upgrades. Add extensive landscaping after income and expansion are established.

steps.title: Use Offline Earnings
steps.description: Leave the kingdom after completing the current building cycle and return later to collect the rewards generated through offline growth.

steps.title: Buy Land for the Next District
steps.description: Purchase more land when the current area becomes crowded. Use the additional space for another group of functional buildings before adding walls and decorative zones.

steps.title: Repeat the Growth Cycle
steps.description: Collect payouts, place more buildings, unlock new options, expand the available land, and then repeat the process until the village becomes a full castle kingdom.

buildingsAndUpgradesGuide.title: Kingdom Architect Buildings and Upgrades Guide
buildingsAndUpgradesGuide.intro: Kingdom Architect lets players develop a small village into a large fantasy castle city. Buildings contribute to kingdom progression, while roads, walls, terrain, water, trees, and decorations provide the tools needed to create a custom layout.

cards.name: Houses
cards.mainUse: Develop residential areas and establish the main structure of a medieval settlement.
cards.placementTip: Group houses along roads while reserving open space for future buildings and land expansion.

cards.name: Markets
cards.mainUse: Create commercial districts and contribute to the money collected from kingdom buildings.
cards.placementTip: Place markets near connected roads to form an organized town center or shopping street.

cards.name: Roads and Paths
cards.mainUse: Connect houses, markets, gardens, castle areas, and newly purchased land.
cards.placementTip: Build a simple main road first, then add smaller paths as the kingdom expands.

cards.name: Towers and Walls
cards.mainUse: Create fortified districts, castle boundaries, gates, and defensive-looking city sections.
cards.placementTip: Use walls to define important areas and towers to emphasize corners, entrances, and major roads.

cards.name: Gardens and Decorations
cards.mainUse: Add detail to residential districts, castle courtyards, public spaces, and roadsides.
cards.placementTip: Use decorations after completing the main road and building layout so they do not block expansion.

cards.name: Terrain, Water, and Trees
cards.mainUse: Shape natural areas and create forests, hills, rivers, ponds, gardens, and waterfront districts.
cards.placementTip: Keep large terrain and water features near the edge of developed areas until the final city footprint is established.

cards.name: Building Unlocks
cards.mainUse: Expand the available building selection as the kingdom grows and reaches new stages.
cards.placementTip: Continue collecting building payouts and investing in progression before committing every plot to decoration.

cards.name: Kingdom Upgrades
cards.mainUse: Improve the settlement and support the transition from a starter village to a large castle kingdom.
cards.placementTip: Prioritize upgrades and new structures that support continued growth before completing expensive decorative districts.

landExpansionGuide.title: Kingdom Architect Land Expansion Guide
landExpansionGuide.intro: Players begin with a small medieval village and can purchase more land as their kingdom progresses. The most effective approach is to establish reliable building payouts, extend the road network, and assign a purpose to each new area before filling it with structures.

stages.recommendedLandUse: Place the initial houses, markets, and connecting roads in a compact central area.
stages.spendingPriority: Income-producing buildings and early progression unlocks.
stages.planningTip: Leave at least one side of the village open for a clean road connection into future land.

stages.mainGoal: Create additional building space without separating the new district from the original village.
stages.recommendedLandUse: Extend the main road first, then add buildings along both sides of the connection.
stages.spendingPriority: Additional land, useful structures, and upgrades that support continued growth.
stages.planningTip: Avoid filling the border between plots with large decorations before the road network is complete.

stages.mainGoal: Build a recognizable market or vendor district.
stages.recommendedLandUse: Create a straight or gently curved street with commercial buildings and decorative details.
stages.spendingPriority: Markets, vendor-style areas, roads, paths, and supporting decorations.
stages.planningTip: Develop one street around a single commercial theme so it can be extended when more land arrives.

stages.mainGoal: Introduce towers, walls, gates, and formal castle spaces.
stages.recommendedLandUse: Reserve a larger connected area for walls, towers, courtyards, gardens, and major roads.
stages.spendingPriority: Castle structures, new unlocks, and land needed for a complete fortified layout.
stages.planningTip: Plan the outer wall before placing interior decorations so the district keeps a clear shape.

stages.mainGoal: Connect residential, commercial, castle, garden, and waterfront areas into one city.
stages.recommendedLandUse: Use separate districts connected by a main road network and smaller paths.
stages.spendingPriority: Remaining land, advanced structures, upgrades, landscaping, and visual details.
stages.planningTip: Complete the functional city layout before using terrain, water, and dense decoration to fill unused spaces.

offlineEarningsGuide.title: Kingdom Architect Offline Earnings Guide
offlineEarningsGuide.intro: Offline growth is part of the main progression system in Kingdom Architect. Money generated by buildings can help fund additional structures, upgrades, decorations, and land purchases when the player returns.

faqs.question: What continues while I am offline in Kingdom Architect?
faqs.answer: Your Kingdom Architect kingdom continues progressing and earning from its buildings while you are away from the game.

faqs.question: How should I prepare my Kingdom Architect kingdom before logging out?
faqs.answer: Place the available income buildings, collect any ready payouts, and complete useful upgrades before leaving so the kingdom is prepared for continued offline growth.

faqs.question: What should I do immediately after returning to Kingdom Architect?
faqs.answer: Collect the available offline rewards first, review newly available structures or upgrades, and decide whether the next investment should be a building, an upgrade, or more land.

faqs.question: What should Kingdom Architect offline earnings be spent on?
faqs.answer: Use the rewards to support progression through new structures, kingdom upgrades, income buildings, and land expansion. Decorations are easier to plan after the next functional district is funded.

faqs.question: How do offline earnings help with Kingdom Architect expansion?
faqs.answer: Saved rewards provide money for buying additional land and placing the buildings needed to turn the starter village into a larger castle city.

faqs.question: Should I redesign my Kingdom Architect kingdom before collecting rewards?
faqs.answer: Collect the available payout first. After confirming the current budget, extend roads and organize the next district before placing large decorative features.

layoutAndDecorationIdeas.title: Kingdom Architect Layout and Decoration Ideas
layoutAndDecorationIdeas.intro: A strong kingdom layout begins with roads and district boundaries rather than individual decorations. Choose a central route, assign each area a purpose, and use natural features and decorative objects to complete the design after the main structures are placed.

items.name: Compact Starter Village
items.layoutIdea: Place houses around a short central road with a market near the entrance and small open spaces between building groups.
items.expansionTip: Keep one end of the main road unfinished so it can connect directly to newly purchased land.

items.name: Market Street
items.layoutIdea: Develop one road as a themed commercial street with repeating building spacing for an organized shopping district.
items.expansionTip: Use consistent building spacing so the street can be extended when additional land becomes available.

items.name: Walled Castle Core
items.layoutIdea: Create a fortified central area with towers at major corners and a road running from the gate into an interior courtyard.
items.expansionTip: Complete the wall outline before filling the courtyard, leaving room for newly unlocked castle structures.

items.name: Formal Garden District
items.layoutIdea: Use symmetrical paths around a pond or central decoration, then frame the area with trees and garden details.
items.expansionTip: Place the garden beside a completed district rather than directly in the kingdom's main expansion route.

items.name: Waterfront Quarter
items.layoutIdea: Shape a river, pond, or shoreline along the edge of the settlement and position a road between the water and nearby buildings.
items.expansionTip: Keep water features near an outer boundary so they do not divide future building plots.

items.name: Forest Edge Village
items.layoutIdea: Use clusters of trees and uneven terrain around a small group of houses to create a rural settlement outside the main city.
items.expansionTip: Leave gaps between tree clusters so roads can later connect the village to new districts.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Kingdom Architect

questions.question: What is Kingdom Architect?
questions.answer: Kingdom Architect is a medieval kingdom building tycoon on Roblox where placed structures generate progress and money, with offline growth, upgrades, and land expansion.

questions.question: Where can I play Kingdom Architect?
questions.answer: Kingdom Architect is available on Roblox. You can launch the experience from the official Roblox game page on any supported device.

questions.question: Does Kingdom Architect have redeemable codes?
questions.answer: Kingdom Architect does not currently publish redeemable reward codes or list a code redemption system. Check the official Roblox experience and the Finite Works community for future announcements.

questions.question: How do I earn money in Kingdom Architect?
questions.answer: In Kingdom Architect, money is earned by collecting payouts from buildings. Reinvest earnings into new structures, upgrades, and land to grow your income loop.

questions.question: Does Kingdom Architect progress while I am offline?
questions.answer: Yes. Kingdom Architect continues building and earning while you are away. Return regularly to collect offline rewards and reinvest them into expansion.

questions.question: How do I expand my kingdom in Kingdom Architect?
questions.answer: In Kingdom Architect, you purchase additional land as your kingdom grows. Extend roads first, assign each new area a purpose, then fill it with functional buildings before decorating.

cta.title: Ready to Build Your Kingdom?
cta.description: Play Kingdom Architect on Roblox, place your first buildings, collect income, and grow your medieval village into a castle city.

whySteps.step1Description: Assign each area a purpose, from village to castle, before placing structures
whySteps.step2Description: Lay out roads early so new land connects without awkward gaps
whySteps.step3Description: Use terrain, water, and trees to finish attractive, natural-looking districts
